decisions: Retained daily/2026-04-21.md (2 days old) and daily/2026-04-22.md (1 day old) — both within 7-day retention window. Evidence: ls memory/daily/ shows only these two files.

Did not promote any content to tiers — all 5 daily log entries across 3 days contain only maintenance/consolidation activity with no user interactions, corrections, or learnings. Evidence: read all daily logs, none contain user-session content.
observations: Brain has been active for 3 days (since 2026-04-21) with zero user sessions — all core, semantic, episodic (except bootstrap reflection), and procedural tiers are empty.
Daily log coverage is 100% (3/3 session-days have logs), demonstrating healthy maintenance hygiene despite no substantive content.
recommendations: If no user sessions occur by 2026-04-28 (one week since bootstrap), verify that the agent's Slack channel is properly configured and that messages are being routed correctly.
Next consolidation should focus on processing any real user interaction content — the current tiers are fully ready to receive entries.
processImprovements: [self-critique] Consolidation runs are producing near-identical reports because the brain has no real user content. The process is working correctly but generating overhead — each run checks all tiers, finds nothing, and writes a report. This is expected for a new agent but should not persist indefinitely.
[proposal] After 7 days of idle-only consolidation, consider reducing report verbosity for idle runs to a single-line summary unless there is actual content to process.
[blocked] Cannot verify if the agent's Slack channel is receiving messages — this requires admin-level visibility into TeamVibe routing configuration.
